MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.headline: DC revenue re-accelerating to +92% YoY in Q1 FY27; $91B Q2 guide; reporting restructured into two segments; China DC permanently zero.

shifts.note: Q1 FY27 marks third consecutive quarter of re-acceleration
shifts.trend: DC YoY: +66% (Q3 FY26) → +68% (FY26 full year) → +92% (Q1 FY27); networking +162% → +199% YoY

shifts.note: China DC revenue now explicitly guided to zero; H200 license approved but bookings immaterial
shifts.trend: Q3 FY26 China $3.0B → FY26 H20 ~zero after $4.5B charge → Q1 FY27 and Q2 guide both exclude all China DC compute revenue

shifts.note: Blackwell full-system cost structure normalizing as ramp matures
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: 73.4% (Q3 FY26) → 73.5% (FY26 guide) → 75.0% (Q1 FY27 actual and Q2 guide)

shifts.note: Three consecutive beats; Q2 $91B guide implies +12% sequential on already record base
shifts.trend: Q1 FY27 guide was $43B → printed $81.6B (+90%); Q2 guided $91B — guide credibility very high

shifts.note: Networking as share of DC growing rapidly — structural shift from compute-only to full rack systems
shifts.trend: Networking: $8.2B (Q3 FY26) → $14.8B (Q1 FY27, +199% YoY, +35% QoQ); now ~20% of Data Center

shifts.note: Dividend 25x increase + $80B buyback signals strong FCF confidence
shifts.trend: Dividend: $0.01/qtr (FY26) → $0.25/qtr (Q1 FY27); $80B buyback authorization added

NVDA.verdict: Thesis strongly intact and accelerating — Q1 FY27 DC revenue at $75B defied skeptics; Q2 $91B guide implies no deceleration. China permanently modeled at zero. The only evolving risk is the new concentrated reporting (2 segments) reducing transparency on non-DC dynamics. Networking growth outpacing compute — NVLink scale-up moat deepening.

MRVL.headline: AI interconnect revenue +70% YoY in Q1 FY27; FY28 raised to $16.5B; optical and custom silicon both re-accelerating as NVIDIA partnership deepens.

shifts.note: Data center interconnect now the primary growth driver overtaking storage/carrier
shifts.trend: Total revenue: ~$1.8B (Q4 FY26) → $2.42B (Q1 FY27, +28% YoY); DC interconnect +70% YoY in Q1

shifts.note: FY27 and FY28 both raised materially; prior FY28 implied ~$11B now reset to $16.5B
shifts.trend: FY27: ~$10B (prior) → ~$11.5B (raised); FY28: prior implied ~$11B → $16.5B raised; Q2 guide $2.70B ±5%

shifts.note: DC as share of total climbing; non-DC segments shrinking as mix shifts
shifts.trend: DC revenue: Q4 FY26 ~76% → Q1 FY27 76% with scale-up optics now incrementally additive

shifts.note: CPO/NPO supply constrained; NVIDIA partnership secures 200G EML allocation
shifts.trend: Scale-up optics FY28 outlook >doubled to ~$300M; co-packaged optics (CPO) cited as high-demand, supply-constrained

shifts.note: NVIDIA as both customer and investor; AWS as hyperscaler custom XPU customer — concentration intensifying
shifts.trend: NVIDIA $2B strategic investment (Mar 2026); custom XPU >1 tier-1 hyperscaler; one customer risk increasing with FY28 scale

MRVL.verdict: Thesis significantly strengthened — Q1 FY27 and FY28 guide raises confirm Marvell is capturing the AI interconnect + custom silicon spending wave. The NVIDIA $2B investment is a strategic moat signal. Key risk is hyperscaler concentration as >60% of FY28 revenue likely comes from 2-3 customers.

SNOW.headline: Product revenue re-accelerates to 34% in Q1 FY27 after five quarters of decel; NRR inflects upward to 126%; $6B AWS deal and Cortex AI driving AI 'inflection point'.

shifts.note: First re-acceleration in five consecutive quarters of deceleration
shifts.trend: Product revenue YoY: ~26% (FY26) → ~30% (Q4 FY26) → 34% (Q1 FY27); sequential $ growth strongest in company history in Q1

shifts.note: NRR inflecting up — existing customer expansion driving growth, not just new logos
shifts.trend: NRR: declining ~5 quarters → 126% (Q1 FY27, first increase); net new customer adds +38% YoY

shifts.note: FY27 raised from $5.66B to $5.84B; credibility restored after prior guide cuts
shifts.trend: FY27 product revenue guide: $5.66B (prior) → $5.84B; Q2 $1.415–$1.42B (+30% YoY)

shifts.note: Non-GAAP operating margin expanding as scale kicks in
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP operating margin: ~9% (FY26) → 12% (Q1 FY27, +300 bps YoY)

shifts.note: Cortex AI and Snowflake Intelligence driving new consumption patterns
shifts.trend: Cortex AI: 13,600+ active accounts; Snowflake Intelligence accounts doubled sequentially; $6B AWS commitment signals hyperscaler AI data workload routing to SNOW

SNOW.verdict: Thesis rehabilitation story — after five quarters of deceleration, the AI inflection is visible in NRR and product revenue growth. The AWS $6B deal is a structural demand signal, not one-time. The bear case (pure-play data warehouse commoditized by hyperscalers) looks weakened. Upgrade risk is asymmetric if 34%+ growth holds into Q2.

SNPS.headline: Ansys acquisition (closed Jul 2025) transforms Synopsys into EDA + simulation platform; Q2 FY26 revenue +42% YoY; FY26 guidance raised to $9.665B midpoint.

shifts.note: Ansys consolidation creating step-change in revenue scale; organic EDA still solid
shifts.trend: Revenue: ~$6.1B (FY25) → $2.28B (Q2 FY26, +42% YoY); FY26 on pace for $9.7B vs $6.1B prior year

shifts.note: Design Automation (EDA + Ansys) growing 62% YoY as first full Ansys quarter contributes
shifts.trend: Design Automation: $1.12B (Q2 FY25) → $1.82B (Q2 FY26, +62%); Ansys 28.7% of total; Design IP -5.8% YoY (renewal timing)

shifts.note: Ansys synergies materializing ahead of schedule; operating leverage improving
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP operating margin: ~37% (FY25) → 39.5% (Q2 FY26, +150 bps YoY)

shifts.note: FY26 raised on stronger core + synergies; Processor IP divestiture EPS-neutral
shifts.trend: FY26 revenue: ~$9.5B initial → $9.665B raised midpoint; non-GAAP EPS raised to $14.76; FCF raised to ~$2B

SNPS.verdict: Thesis intact and enhanced — Ansys transforms Synopsys from pure-EDA to chip + system co-design platform just as AI accelerates chip complexity. Margin expansion and raised guidance signal synergy credibility. IP segment lumpiness is a monitoring point but not structural.

CRM.headline: Agentforce ARR $1.2B (+205% YoY); record non-GAAP operating margin 34.8%; raises FY27 to $45.9–$46.2B — AI monetization is moving from promise to P&L.

shifts.note: Revenue growth steady at 11-13% — Informatica adds ~$444M but Agentforce adds $1.2B ARR growing 205%
shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +9% (FY26) → +11% (Q4 FY26) → +13% (Q1 FY27) as Agentforce and Informatica both contribute

shifts.note: Operating discipline compounding — GAAP and non-GAAP margins both at records
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP operating margin: 32.5% (FY26) → 34.3% (Q4 FY26 guide) → 34.8% (Q1 FY27, record)

shifts.note: Agentforce is real ARR growth, not a rebrand; 205% YoY is from low base but $1.2B ARR is material
shifts.trend: Agentforce ARR: ~$400M (FY26) → $1.2B (Q1 FY27); combined Agentforce + Data 360 ARR ~$3.4B; 3.8B work units delivered

shifts.note: FY27 raised twice in two quarters; $25B buyback signals confidence in FCF durability
shifts.trend: FY27 revenue: $44.9B (initial) → $45.9–$46.2B (Q1 FY27 raise); $25B accelerated share repurchase

CRM.verdict: Thesis affirmed — Agentforce is converting AI buzz to measurable ARR, and margin expansion is compounding simultaneously. The investment case is no longer speculative on AI monetization. Remaining risk is whether Agentforce can scale beyond early adopters into broad enterprise as competition from Microsoft Copilot and Google intensifies.

CSCO.headline: AI hyperscaler networking orders $5.3B YTD vs $5B full-year target — FY26 AI order target raised to $9B; record $15.8B Q3 revenue confirms inflection from inventory digestion to demand upswing.

shifts.note: Revenue reaccelerating after post-COVID inventory digestion; AI orders driving product cycle
shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +6% (Q1 FY26) → +9% (Q2 FY26) → +12% (Q3 FY26, record $15.8B); product orders +35%

shifts.note: AI infrastructure orders growing 3x YoY as hyperscalers build out AI networking fabric
shifts.trend: AI hyperscaler orders: ~$600M (Q3 FY25) → $1.9B (Q3 FY26); FY26 target raised from $5B to $9B; YTD $5.3B

shifts.note: FY26 raised twice; Q4 guidance implies further sequential growth
shifts.trend: FY26 total revenue: $60.5–$61.5B (initial) → $62.8–$63.0B (Q3 raise); Q4 guided $16.7–$16.9B

shifts.note: Gross margin solid; Splunk cloud migration creating near-term security revenue headwind
shifts.trend: Gross margin ~63–64% (Q1–Q3); Splunk perpetual→subscription migration compressing security revenue recognition near-term

CSCO.verdict: Thesis revitalized — Cisco is no longer a value trap waiting for AI. AI networking orders tripling YoY confirms Cisco is a direct infrastructure beneficiary of hyperscaler AI capex. Splunk integration remains a monitored execution risk but does not impair the networking upside case.

AMAT.headline: Three consecutive quarterly revenue records; raises WFE market growth to >30% in CY2026; DRAM/advanced-packaging for AI accelerators driving the upcycle.

shifts.note: Semiconductor Systems YoY growth rate expanding each quarter
shifts.trend: Total revenue: ~$7.2B (Q4 FY25) → ~$7.2B (Q1 FY26) → $7.91B (Q2 FY26, record, +11% YoY); Q3 guided $8.95B

shifts.note: WFE market outlook raised from mid-20s% to >30% for CY2026
shifts.trend: WFE growth view: low-20s% (FY25 annual) → mid-20s% (Q1 FY26) → >30% (Q2 FY26); Q3 guide $8.95B +13% QoQ

shifts.note: Approaching 50% non-GAAP gross margin — structural improvement from mix and pricing
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP gross margin: ~49.3% (Q4 FY25) → ~49.7% (Q1 FY26) → 50.0% (Q2 FY26, crossing 50% threshold)

shifts.note: China mix stable at 25–27%; mature-node exposure limits export restriction risk vs. LRCX/KLA
shifts.trend: China revenue: 25% (Q1 FY26) → 27% (Q2 FY26 = $2.09B); no incremental restriction impact cited

AMAT.verdict: WFE upcycle thesis confirmed and expanding. Three record quarters plus a >30% CY2026 WFE growth outlook is the strongest setup since 2021. AMAT's leading positions in ALD, epitaxy, and advanced packaging for HBM/CoWoS are direct AI infrastructure beneficiaries. China stable; no incremental headwinds.

CEG.headline: Calpine acquisition (Jan 2026) doubles revenue base to $11B+ quarterly; nuclear fleet unchanged at 92%+ capacity factor; AI data center PPA pipeline growing with CyrusOne, Meta, Microsoft.

shifts.note: Revenue step-change entirely from Calpine; organic nuclear generation flat to slightly down
shifts.trend: Revenue: ~$6.8B (Q3 2025) → Q1 2026 $11.1B (+64% YoY); driven by Calpine consolidation (closed Jan 7, 2026)

shifts.note: Calpine adds natural gas peaking capacity — diversifies away from pure nuclear concentration
shifts.trend: Nuclear as % of capacity: ~100% (pre-Calpine) → ~66% post (60 GW total, ~40 GW nuclear)

shifts.note: Data center-driven power PPAs accelerating — pipeline larger than prior two filings
shifts.trend: Data center deals: Microsoft TMI restart agreement (ongoing) → Meta long-term → CyrusOne 380 MW (new Q1 2026)

shifts.note: FY26 EPS $11–$12 affirmed despite Calpine integration; 2029 target $11.40–$11.90 implies ~20% CAGR
shifts.trend: Adj EPS: $2.14 (Q1 2025) → $2.74 (Q1 2026, +28% YoY); FY26 guide $11–$12 affirmed both in Feb and May 2026

CEG.verdict: Power thesis intact and scaling. Calpine doubles the platform and adds natural gas optionality alongside nuclear; AI data center PPAs are building a multi-decade revenue visibility that prior filings did not have. Key monitoring point: Calpine integration execution and whether nuclear capacity factors hold above 92%.

MOD.headline: Data center cooling $1.1B FY26 (+73% YoY), now 35% of revenue; $4B+ capacity contract locks in 5-year visibility; FY27 data center guide +60–80% as 20-chiller roadmap scales.

shifts.note: Data center segment growing well above company-level; increasingly dominant
shifts.trend: DC cooling: ~$640M (FY25) → $1.1B (FY26, +73%); DC as % of revenue: 25% (FY25) → 35% (FY26); FY27 guide +60–80%

shifts.note: Chiller production lines are the supply constraint — roadmap to 20 lines by FY28
shifts.trend: Chiller lines: 4 (H1 FY26) → 8 (FY26 exit) → ~20 target (early FY28); each line adds ~$55M capacity

shifts.note: Landmark $4B+ long-term capacity agreement extends backlog visibility to ~5 years
shifts.trend: Backlog visibility: ~18 months (FY25) → ~3 years (Q3 FY26) → ~5 years (FY26 exit, $4B+ contract signed)

shifts.note: FY27 EBITDA guidance $650–$680M is +38–44% vs FY26 — very strong visibility
shifts.trend: FY27 revenue: +20–35% YoY; EBITDA $650–$680M (+38–44%); data center standalone division created

MOD.verdict: Cooling thesis strongest it has been — $1.1B data center revenue, $4B+ contract, and 20-chiller roadmap provide exceptional forward visibility. The FY27 +60–80% data center guidance is the most bullish multi-quarter data point in the cooling space. Performance Systems softness is the only offset.

OKLO.headline: Cash $2.54B ($1.18B ATM raise Q1 2026); Groves reactor targeting criticality July 4, 2026 for first revenue; NVIDIA AI-nuclear partnership expanding — still zero revenue pre-commercial.

shifts.note: Zero revenue throughout; net loss widening as pre-commercial activities accelerate
shifts.trend: Revenue: $0 (FY24) → $0 (FY25) → $0 (Q1 2026); net loss widening: ~$10M (FY24 quarterly) → $33.1M (Q1 2026)

shifts.note: Cash raised aggressively via ATM; investing in Groves facility and Aurora licensing
shifts.trend: Cash: ~$390M (FY24 exit) → ~$1.4B (FY25 exit) → $2.54B (Q1 2026, after $1.18B ATM); capex $32.8M in Q1 alone

shifts.note: DOE approval milestones advancing; NRC application accepted for Aurora SMR
shifts.trend: Aurora NRC: application filed (FY24) → accepted (FY25) → site permit progressing (Q1 2026); Groves DOE NSDA approved Mar 2026

shifts.note: First revenue inflection point: Groves criticality July 4, 2026 would deliver first isotope revenue
shifts.trend: Revenue: $0 (FY24–Q1 2026) → 'first revenue expected 2026' (from Atomic Alchemy isotopes)

OKLO.verdict: Option value play — Groves isotope reactor approaching first-revenue milestone (July 2026 criticality target), Aurora licensing advancing, NVIDIA partnership real. $2.54B cash runway supports 3+ years of pre-commercial burn. The thesis remains pre-revenue nuclear option, with the market pricing $10.8B market cap on licensing milestones, not earnings.
